inline_comments.message:
These tags look out of place. 

We need to put all dependencies described below in between 
<dependencyManagement>  ... </dependencyManagement>

Not sure I agree on this one, Jess.

As I understand it, the <dependencyManagement> tag is used to account for dependencies that might be used in child projects (which is not applicable here).  My read (and experiment) also confirm that I need local dependencies to be listed in an independent <dependenies> tag (ie. not one within <dependencyManagement>).

My pom file is generated by an automated process.  I agree that it's a bit odd it should go out of its way to create an empty <dependencyManagement> structure - but it's ultimately harmless, and I think the local dependencies are placed correctly.

Open to further thoughts from you or others (and to correction on any point), but I think this section should be left to stand as is.

Thanks.

Thank you for the explanation. 

I didn't knew it was generated automatically. 
I am slightly familiar with this process overall but I will trust your judgement :)

Jess
